wrapUp: Matchday four reshuffled the favourites' narrative. Spain were held 0-0 by Cape Verde despite Lamine Yamal's second-half introduction, Brazil drew 1-1 with Morocco without Neymar, and the Netherlands twice surrendered the lead in a 2-2 opener with Japan. Australia stunned Türkiye 2-0 to join the United States atop Group D, Tunisia sacked Sabri Lamouchi after a 5-1 defeat to Sweden, and Argentina prepare to defend their title on 16 June without Nicolas Tagliafico but with Lionel Messi passed fit. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 15 June 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: Tagliafico ruled out as Messi passed fit for Algeria opener
stories.summary: Lyon left-back Nicolas Tagliafico will miss Argentina's Group J curtain-raiser against Algeria on 16 June with a calf strain, with Facundo Medina or Valentin Barco expected to deputise. Lionel Messi, meanwhile, is on track for his 200th cap after managing a hamstring issue through the warm-up window, and Emiliano Martinez has recovered from a broken finger to reclaim the number one shirt. Leandro Paredes and Nicolas Gonzalez are also fit, but Tagliafico's absence may extend to the Austria fixture on 22 June.

stories.headline: Yamal off the bench but Spain held by Cape Verde in opener
stories.summary: Luis de la Fuente started without Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams in Atlanta on 15 June, with both eased back from hamstring injuries, and even Yamal's 71st-minute introduction could not break down Cape Verde in a 0-0 draw. De la Fuente had said Yamal was fit to play part of the match but not 90 minutes; the teenager still led all players with five dribbles after coming on. Euro 2024 winners remain without Fermín López, who fractured a metatarsal in May, and must sharpen their attack before facing Saudi Arabia on 21 June.

stories.headline: Brazil draw with Morocco without Neymar as Haiti return looms
stories.summary: Vinícius Júnior equalised in a 1-1 draw with Morocco on 13 June, but Brazil created without Neymar, who missed the opener with a grade-two calf injury and is targeting a return to training this week ahead of the Haiti fixture on 19 June. Carlo Ancelotti said Neymar may rejoin the group next week, though a starting role against Haiti still looks unlikely after a month out. Brazil have already lost Rodrygo, Estêvão and Wesley before the tournament, leaving Ancelotti's attack thinner than their pedigree suggests.

stories.headline: Pulisic plays down calf knock after precautionary half-time withdrawal
stories.summary: Christian Pulisic was substituted at half-time during the United States' 4-1 win over Paraguay on 12 June after taking a kick to his left calf on top of a pre-existing hamstring strain. The AC Milan forward and Mauricio Pochettino both described the change as precautionary, with Pulisic telling reporters it was "nothing" serious. He had set up Folarin Balogun's first goal and was the team's main creative outlet in a dominant opening 45 minutes, so any setback would be felt before the Australia fixture on 19 June.

stories.headline: Canada earn first World Cup point without Davies
stories.summary: Co-hosts Canada drew 1-1 with Bosnia and Herzegovina at BMO Field on 12 June, Cyle Larin equalising in the 78th minute after Jovo Lukic had headed Bosnia ahead. Alphonso Davies did not feature as he continues to recover from a hamstring injury sustained in May, though Jesse Marsch said an MRI showed encouraging progress and Davies may return for the Qatar fixture on 18 June. Centre-back Moïse Bombito was available after winning his fitness race. The point was Canada's first in World Cup history, ending a run of six consecutive group-stage defeats.

stories.headline: Richards starts as United States thump Paraguay 4-1
stories.summary: Johnny Cardoso was ruled out after ankle surgery, but Mauricio Pochettino named Chris Richards at centre-back for the 4-1 Group D opener on 12 June, the Crystal Palace defender having recovered from torn ankle ligaments in May. Folarin Balogun scored twice in a joint-record World Cup win for the hosts. Pochettino had confirmed all 26 remaining players were available beforehand, with Group D also featuring Australia and Türkiye.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Argentina lose Balerdi as Senesi confirmed as replacement
stories.summary: Leonardo Balerdi suffered a soleus muscle tear in training and the AFA confirmed he would miss the World Cup. On 12 June, the federation announced that Marcos Senesi would take his place, ending speculation that Lionel Scaloni might recall a midfielder instead. Senesi, who has joined Tottenham on a free transfer, adds cover alongside Cristian Romero, Lisandro Martínez and Nicolás Otamendi. Argentina open Group J against Algeria on 16 June.

stories.headline: Endo withdraws and retires as Machino called up before Netherlands opener
stories.summary: Japan captain Wataru Endo pulled out of the World Cup on 11 June after failing to recover from a foot injury sustained at Liverpool in February, and announced his immediate retirement from international football with 73 caps. Borussia Mönchengladbach forward Shūto Machino was named as his replacement, while Ajax defender Kō Itakura takes over as captain. Japan had already lost Kaoru Mitoma and Takumi Minamino to injury, thinning a midfield that drew 2-2 with the Netherlands in Dallas on 14 June.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: Tunisia sack Lamouchi after 5-1 Sweden defeat
stories.summary: Sabri Lamouchi was dismissed on 15 June, the day after Tunisia's 5-1 opening defeat to Sweden in Monterrey, with Mondher Kebaier installed as interim coach ahead of the Japan fixture on 21 June. Lamouchi had already faced pressure after a 5-0 warm-up loss to Belgium and was criticised for having his son at the training camp despite no official role. Tunisia must now regroup in a Group F pool where Japan top the standings after drawing with the Netherlands and Sweden sit second after their five-goal haul.

stories.headline: England training gear stolen en route to Kansas City base
stories.summary: Balls, boots and other equipment were stolen from a vehicle transferring England's kit from their Florida pre-tournament camp to Swope Soccer Village in Kansas City on 13 June, just before the squad were due to arrive. Kansas City police arrested two suspects and most items were subsequently recovered, but the incident disrupted setup ahead of Thomas Tuchel's first full training day on 14 June. England open Group L against Croatia in Dallas on 17 June.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: Cape Verde hold Spain as favourites stumble on matchday four
stories.summary: Debutants Cape Verde earned a 0-0 draw against Spain in Atlanta despite registering just three shots on target, with 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha making seven saves. Spain dominated possession but lacked the incision of Yamal and Williams, who were both managed off the bench, echoing their meek knockout exits in 2018 and 2022. Opta data showed 27 Spanish attempts in total, yet Group H suddenly looks less straightforward with Saudi Arabia and Uruguay still to come.

stories.headline: Depleted Brazil and Morocco share points in Group C opener
stories.summary: Ismael Saibari gave Morocco a 21st-minute lead before Vinícius Júnior levelled in a 1-1 draw that reflected both sides' injury disruption. Brazil were without Neymar, Rodrygo, Estêvão and Wesley, while Morocco had replaced Ezzalzouli and Aguerd before kick-off. Neither team found a winner despite 27 combined attempts and eight on target, leaving Haiti and Scotland to fight for scraps as Brazil and Morocco turn to supposedly softer fixtures on matchday two.

stories.headline: Australia stun Türkiye to join United States atop Group D
stories.summary: Nestory Irankunda and Connor Metcalfe scored as the Socceroos beat Türkiye 2-0 in Vancouver on 14 June despite conceding 71.7% possession and facing 30 shots. Debutant goalkeeper Patrick Beach made eight saves to keep a clean sheet, leaving Australia level on points with the United States ahead of their Seattle meeting on 19 June. Türkiye, back at a World Cup for the first time since 2002, must regroup against Paraguay after dominating territorially without reward.

stories.headline: Japan fight back twice to deny depleted Netherlands in Dallas
stories.summary: Ronald Koeman's side twice led through Virgil van Dijk and Crysencio Summerville but were pegged back by Keito Nakamura and a late Daichi Kamada header in a 2-2 draw on 14 June. Bart Verbruggen started despite a hip concern, yet the Dutch failed to win a World Cup match against an Asian nation for the first time in six attempts. Japan top Group F on disciplinary tiebreakers after Sweden's 5-1 win over Tunisia, setting up a pool where squad depth and composure under pressure may decide who advances.
